results.query: Exact Sciences Riskguard product page hereditary cancer genetic test

results.title: [PDF] Riskguard Hereditary Cancer Test Technical Specifications
results.content: Riskguard Hereditary Cancer Test Technical Specifications Prevention Genetics LLC part of Exact Sciences Effective date: September 2025 The Riskguard Hereditary Cancer Test is intended for the identification of germline variants in genes known to be associated with an elevated risk for cancer susceptibility. The results of this test are intended to aid healthcare professionals in cancer risk assessment and decisions regarding cancer screening, risk reducing interventions, and medical management. This test is not appropriate for the detection of somatic variants. Indication for use and intended patient population The Riskguard Hereditary Cancer Test detects germline substitutions, insertions/deletions, and copy number variants in the coding regions (+/- 10bp flanking regions unless [...] section below. Description of analysis Genomic DNA is extracted from peripheral blood, saliva or buccal samples. The DNA corresponding to target regions is captured using custom hybridization probes. Captured DNA is sequenced by next generation sequencing methods (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA). Description of methods 1 Exact Sciences | Riskguard® Hereditary Cancer Test Variants are aligned using human reference genome (hg19, GRCh37 Genomic Reference Consortium Human Reference 37 [GCA_000001405.1]). Variant calling is performed using a combination of open-source and laboratory developed pipelines. Variants that do not meet quality metrics require confirmatory testing that includes various orthogonal methods, including multiplex ligation-dependent probe amplification (MLPA), Sanger [...] Yes Yes RAD51C NM_058216.2 Yes Yes RAD51D NM_002878.3 Yes Yes 5 Exact Sciences | Riskguard® Hereditary Cancer Test The Riskguard test and its performance characteristics have been developed and validated by PreventionGenetics LLC, an Exact Sciences laboratory. The test is processed in Marshfield, WI at PreventionGenetics, who is qualified to perform high-complexity clinical laboratory testing per the College of American Pathologists (CAP; license #7185561), Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA: license #52D2065132), and ISO15189 (#3850.01) regulations. The Riskguard test has not been cleared or approved by the US Food and Drug Administration or other notified regulatory authority. © 2025 PreventionGenetics LLC. All rights reserved. Riskguard and Exact Sciences are trademarks

results.title: Riskguard® Hereditary Cancer Test | Exact Sciences
results.content:
# Riskguard® Hereditary Cancer test

The Riskguard test is an assessment tool that can help uncover hereditary cancer risk. With just a simple blood, saliva or buccal (cheek) swab sample, it checks for hereditary genetic changes that can cause an increased risk for certain cancers.

For providers

See the difference the Riskguard test can make for your patients.

For patients

See the difference the Riskguard test can make for you.

Benefits of the Riskguard test

## Being proactive now can make a difference for the future

### Powerful information about hereditary cancer risk [...] The Exact Sciences Riskguard test was developed, and its performance characteristics validated by an Exact Sciences laboratory following College of American Pathologists (CAP) and Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) regulations. The test is performed in Marshfield, WI at PreventionGenetics LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Exact Sciences. PreventionGenetics is accredited by CAP, certified under CLIA regulations, and qualified to perform high-complexity clinical laboratory testing. It has not been cleared or approved by the US Food and Drug Administration or other notified regulatory authority. Genomic Health, Inc., an Exact Sciences company, is the billing entity for the Riskguard test. [...] ### Results that empower you and your family members

Receive easy-to-understand results with potential next steps that providers and patients can use for personalized care planning.

The Riskguard test can also identify family members at risk and help them make more informed decisions about strategies to reduce their cancer risk.

The Riskguard test checks 59 genes across 11 common cancer types

Approximately 1 in 8 cancers are due to a hereditary syndrome.1 The Riskguard test helps patients learn more about their genetic risk for some of the most common and aggressive cancer types.

Colorectal    
Breast    
Prostate    
Pancreatic    
Ovarian    
Endometrial    
Skin    
Gastric    
Kidney    
Endocrine    
Lung

See Riskguard gene information

## The Riskguard test process

## Who can the Riskguard test help?

A man and woman sitting together with their eyes closed, holding hands.

### Patients without cancer

The Riskguard test helps inform the risk stratification for patients who have a family history of cancer by identifying variants in clinically actionable cancer-associated genes, which may inform a customized care path.

Customize a risk-management path [...] Exact Sciences is now Abbott.
Exact Sciences is now Abbott.
!(/-/media/project/headless/shared/images/flags/us.svg?h=200&iar=0&w=300&rev=37af7af9a7bc47028a5cdb2bfb4fd2fc&hash=625B2D05BF0789F11173E3C99301A18F)

# The Riskguard® test helps you help your patients

A health care provider with stethoscope talking to a patient.
A health care provider with stethoscope talking to a patient.

## Gene panel

## Specimen requirements

## Turnaround time

An older woman smiling and holding a baby, interacting with an older man and a younger woman.

## Harness the power of genetic insights to help personalize treatment and risk-management4-9

A younger man in glasses, kissing the head of an older woman who is smiling.

### Hereditary cancer is common [...] ### Hereditary cancer is common

Hereditary cancers, which account for approximately 10-15% of cancer diagnoses, are caused by pathogenic germline variants and are often associated with early onset cancer, aggressive forms of cancer, and multiple cancer diagnoses in an individual and/or family.1

A health care provider with stethoscope placing her hand on a patient’s shoulder.

### Testing is under-utilized

It is estimated that only 7% of patients with cancer have received germline genetic testing,2 which could inform treatment. And the majority of both diagnosed and undiagnosed patients do not regret undergoing testing and are interested in learning all results from germline genetic testing.3

## Faster genetic insights for informed surgical decisions

Get expedited results from nine high-risk cancer genes in just 7 - 10 days when you order the STAT Breast Cancer Panel with the Riskguard test.

## Access everything all in one place

With Exact Sciences' online Provider Hub, ordering a test, tracking test progress, and accessing results is convenient and efficient. [...] Hands holding a tablet device showing a Riskguard test ordering screen from the Exact Sciences Provider Hub.
